entries.reason: Optional first-key success requires generated compressed public-key bytes to represent a valid P256 curve point.
This operation requires an authenticated WebSocket upgrade; Schemathesis exercises plain HTTP requests only.
This operation requires a WebSocket upgrade; Schemathesis exercises plain HTTP requests only.
Success requires an existing account, registered signer, state-bound nonce, and valid signature or WebAuthn assertion.
Success requires an existing account and API key plus a registered signer and valid state-bound authorization.
Success requires an account allocated by an earlier stateful operation; the contract run does not seed durable identities.
Success requires an existing zero-key account and a generated byte string that is also a valid P256 curve point.
Success requires current account digests, a registered signer, and valid P256 or WebAuthn authorization.
Success requires current account digests, an existing target key, and valid P256 or WebAuthn authorization.
Success requires an existing account, registered signer, fresh nonce, and valid P256 or WebAuthn authorization.
Success requires a configured bridge domain and a deposit disposition consistent with existing account state.
Success requires a configured bridge, existing funded account, fresh nonce, and valid registered-signer authorization.
Success requires the generated compressed public-key bytes to represent a valid P256 curve point and not conflict with state.
Optional resolution templates are deployment-installed state; arbitrary schema-valid names are intentionally rejected when absent.
Success depends on an existing unresolved market and, when supplied, a registered feed with a valid signed attestation.
Success requires generated compressed public-key bytes to represent a valid P256 curve point and consume available account stock.
Success requires an existing funded account, existing tradeable market, and order values satisfying current stateful admission policy.
Success requires an existing owned order plus a registered signer, fresh nonce, and valid cancellation authorization.
Success requires an active owned bundle revision plus a registered signer, fresh nonce, and valid authorization.
Success requires an active owned bundle revision, the exact next-block fence, and valid registered-signer authorization.
Success requires existing funded account and market state, the exact next-block fence, and valid registered-signer authorization.
Success requires existing funded account and market state plus a registered signer, fresh nonce, and valid authorization.
